name: Belarus (presidential standard)

colors.symbolism: the upper band of the field — heritage and the blood of martyrs
the lower band — Belarusian forests and meadows
the hoist ornament and details of the emblem

description: The Belarusian national flag (a red-green horizontal bicolour, two-thirds red over one-third green, with a vertical white-on-red Belarusian ornament along the hoist) defaced in the centre with the state emblem — a green outline of Belarus over a globe and rising sun, surmounted by a red star and framed by ears of wheat with clover and flax.
history: Adopted by presidential decree on 27 March 2008. It places the state emblem (in use since the 1995 referendum that replaced the Pahonia coat of arms) in the centre of the national flag, with a near-square 5:6 proportion distinct from the standard 1:2 national flag.

features.role: Belarusian ornamental band along the hoist (white-on-red ornament)
State Emblem of the Republic of Belarus
